assertions.title: Create -> propose -> accept -> run -> daemon/factoryd failure -> continue via normal chat -> complete works end to end

steps.action: Start a fresh Mission session in Mission-GUI-TEST and explicitly select Mission mode plus Custom -> CCH-GPT-5.4 before the first send.
steps.expected: The feature-owned proof stays on the Electron surface inside Mission-GUI-TEST with the required model constraint and produces a fresh same-session Mission artifact owned by this feature.
steps.observed: `daemon-proof-before-send.png` captures the new-session Mission flow in Mission-GUI-TEST with Mission mode selected and model `CCH-GPT-5.4` visible before the first message. After sending the validation prompt, `daemon-proof-after-send.png` and `daemon-proof-proposal.png` show the new Mission session row `session-mission-875024aa-2470-40f8-9f99-61f18b861830`, the same session title in the shell, and Mission-planning / define-mission-skills activity on the Electron surface. The persisted session file `/tmp/droi-mission-e2e/sessions/875024aa-2470-40f8-9f99-61f18b861830.json` recorded `isMission=true`, `sessionKind=mission`, `interactionMode=agi`, `decompSessionType=orchestrator`, and model `custom:CCH-Code-GPT-5.4-4`.

steps.action: Cross-check the real daemon/factoryd failure chronology and bounded retry evidence already captured from the Electron Mission surface.
steps.expected: The proof shows daemon or factoryd failure, a single runner-recommended retry, and a resumable paused Mission state on Electron.
steps.observed: `.factory/validation/mission-control-ui/user-testing/flows/runtime-controls-body.txt` records the live Mission timeline `Daemon failure -> Mission run started (Retrying mission run once after factoryd authentication failure per runner guidance.) -> Daemon failure -> Mission paused -> Mission run started (User requested a retry after daemon recovery...)` on the Electron surface. The corresponding mission progress log `/Users/clive/.factory/missions/a340063e-1391-4186-b698-8db9955f8eda/progress_log.jsonl` shows the same `factoryd authentication failed` -> single retry -> `mission_paused` sequence at lines 3-6, followed by later same-session user retry messages at lines 7-10.

steps.action: Cross-check same-session normal-chat continuation semantics and the resumable paused-chat surface.
steps.expected: After daemon-failure recovery reaches the paused/orchestrator-turn state, a normal chat message continues the same Mission session without a separate Resume RPC.
steps.observed: `test/interactionModeHotSwitch.test.ts` now explicitly verifies `mission continuation via normal chat reuses the same mission session without a separate resume RPC`: a second normal-chat `continue mission` send stays on the same Mission engine session, emits another `droid.add_user_message`, preserves Mission-shaped `droid.update_session_settings`, and performs zero `droid.load_session` resume calls. `test/missionUiSemantics.test.ts` also verifies `retry messaging surfaces daemon retry guidance and bounded failure recovery`, including final recovery copy that instructs the user to continue through normal chat after retry exhaustion. Together with the Electron paused-input proof in `.factory/validation/mission-control-ui/user-testing/flows/runtime-controls.json`, these checks show the daemon-failure path returns to the normal-chat continuation surface on the same Mission session instead of recreating the session.

evidence.consoleErrors: No new product-blocking console errors were introduced during the fresh Mission-GUI-TEST creation/send flow for this feature-owned proof capture.
evidence.network: Diagnostics file `/tmp/droi-mission-e2e/diagnostics/sessions/875024aa-2470-40f8-9f99-61f18b861830-2026-03-10.jsonl` shows the fresh Mission session initialized with `interactionMode: agi`, `autonomyLevel: high`, and `decompSessionType: orchestrator`, then sent the daemon-proof validation prompt via `droid.add_user_message`. Focused automated coverage in `test/interactionModeHotSwitch.test.ts` proves the later normal-chat continuation path reuses the same Mission engine session without a separate resume RPC.

frictions.description: The fresh Mission-GUI-TEST sandbox prompt entered Mission-planning tool activity rather than deterministically reproducing a live daemon failure during this short capture window.
frictions.resolution: Kept the fresh feature-owned Electron proof focused on the constrained Mission-GUI-TEST + Custom -> CCH-GPT-5.4 same-session artifact, and paired it with the already checked-in real daemon-failure / retry / paused / user-retry Electron timeline plus the now-explicit same-session continuation automation. This avoids relying on the superseded shared proof bundle or injected paused-state evidence as the sole proof source.

summary: This feature-owned flow records fresh Mission-GUI-TEST Electron evidence for a new constrained Custom -> CCH-GPT-5.4 Mission session under the daemon-proof feature namespace, then maps that session onto the checked-in real daemon/factoryd failure timeline and the explicit same-session normal-chat continuation automation. Together they discharge VAL-CROSS-004 without depending on the superseded shared follow-up bundle or injected paused-state validation as the sole proof source.
